2605.12365.title: QAP-Router: Tackling Qubit Routing as Dynamic Quadratic Assignment with Reinforcement Learning
2605.12365.paper_summary: Qubit routing is a fundamental problem in quantum compilation, known to be NP-hard. Its dynamic nature makes local routing decisions propagate and compound over time, making global efficient solutions challenging. Existing heuristic methods rely on local rules with limited lookahead, while recent learning-based approaches often treat routing as a generic sequential decision problem without fully exploiting its underlying structure. In this paper, we introduce QAP-Router, framing qubit routing based on a dynamic Quadratic Assignment Problem (QAP) formulation. By modeling logical interactions, or quantum gates, as flow matrices and hardware topology as a distance matrix, our approach captures the interaction-distance coupling in a unified objective, which defines the reward in the reinforcement learning environment. To further exploit this structure, the policy network employs a solution-aware Transformer backbone that encodes the interaction between the flow matrix and the distance matrix into the attention mechanism. We also integrate a lookahead mechanism that blends naturally into the QAP framework, preventing myopic decisions. Extensive experiments on 1,831 real-world quantum circuits from the MQTBench, AgentQ and QUEKO datasets show that our method substantially reduces the CNOT gate count of routed circuits by 15.7%, 30.4% and 12.1%, respectively, relative to existing industry compilers.
2605.12365.paper_summary_zh: 量子位路由是量子編譯中的一個基本問題，已知為 NP-hard。其動態特性使得局部路由決策隨時間傳播並累積，這使得全球高效解決方案變得具有挑戰性。現有的啟發式方法依賴於有限前瞻的局部規則，而最近的基於學習的方法則常常將路由視為一個通用的序列決策問題，而未充分利用其底層結構。在本文中，我們介紹了 QAP-Router，將量子位路由框架建立在動態二次分配問題 (QAP) 的表述上。通過將邏輯交互或量子閘建模為流矩陣，並將硬體拓撲建模為距離矩陣，我們的方法在統一目標中捕捉了交互-距離耦合，這定義了強化學習環境中的獎勵。為了進一步利用這一結構，策略網絡採用了解決方案感知的 Transformer 主幹，將流矩陣和距離矩陣之間的交互編碼到注意力機制中。我們還整合了一個前瞻機制，這自然融入 QAP 框架中，防止短視的決策。對來自 MQTBench、AgentQ 和 QUEKO 數據集的 1,831 個真實世界量子電路進行的廣泛實驗顯示，我們的方法相對於現有的行業編譯器，顯著減少了路由電路的 CNOT 閘數量，分別為 15.7%、30.4% 和 12.1%。
2605.12365.author: Kien X. Nguyen et.al.
2605.12365.authors: Kien X. Nguyen, Ankit Kulshrestha, Ilya Safro, Xiaoyuan Liu
